title: 30+ Free Resources for Small Businesses and Startups
author: Caylin White

sections.heading: Key Takeaways
sections.text: Learn over 30 free resources on sales, marketing, service, commerce, productivity, AI, and business strategy.From reports on SMB tends to webinars on AI agents, these resources range from business strategy to technology innovations.Free resources like this are helping businesses meet customer expectations 65% better than previous years. As asmall and medium business (SMB)owner, you’re the CEO, social media manager, sales rep, and customer service representative all in one. If there’s one thing that can help you navigate these many roles, it’s knowledge. Always be learning, as the saying goes. Well, we’ve done the heavy lifting for you and compiled 30 free resources across all business teams — sales, marketing, service, and commerce — all with the added bonuses of productivity andartificial intelligence (AI). These valuable resources come from the experts, leaders, and innovators at Salesforce (along with some of our esteemed partners) built on trust, data, and all things AI. Let’s jump right in.

sections.heading: Free resources on sales and growth
sections.text: Learning how to sell your products is the toughest and the most rewarding skill. These resources will equip you with information and tips on sales and business growth: Salesforce’sSmall and Medium Business Trends Reportis a go-to resource for global SMBs andstartupsfor one reason: know what other founders are prioritizing. We spent over a year digging deep into this free report to help SMBs optimize their business forsustainable growth. Get insights intoAI tools for SMBs, top challenges, and tips to navigate them. And learn howAI agentscan improve your business operations. Want to learn how to manage yoursales pipelineor useautomationin your sales journey?Sales for Small Business, a course onTrailhead, Salesforce’s free online learning platform, is your best bet. This short Trail requires only 30 minutes and introduces you to ‌customer relationship management (CRM)for small teams. Here are some key takeaways: Better lead management: ACRM for small businesslikeStarter Suitehelps youmanage your leads, and focus on hot leads that will likely convert.Automation enhances productivity:Pro Suitecan automate multiple tasks and boost your workflows and productivity. Salesforce’sState of Sales Reportdraws insights from 5,500+ sales professionals, covering everything from AI adoption in sales to revenue-generating methods likeupselling, andcross-selling. Learn about the top challenges that sales teams are facing, and how to address them. Here are some key takeaways: Employee retention matters: Improve your team happiness andproductivitythrough regular coaching andsales enablementon new tools, and AI.Good data matters: Sales teams rely on data for tasks likesales forecasting, setting targets, and quotas. Salesforce for Small Businessis a Trailhead module on thebenefits of a CRMfor small and growing teams. In just 10 minutes, get an overview of SMBs’ impact on the economy and how Starter Suite equips them for the future. Here are some key takeaways: CRM benefits: CRMs offer a 360-degree view of your sales and service operations, helping you make informed decisions.Starter Suite’s scalability: Starter Suite grows as you scale, helping you seamlessly manage leads, contacts, and overall growth. Some say selling is an art, but we say it can be learned. TheSMB Sales: A Complete Guideis a comprehensive resource on how to go after your prospects. It dives deep into the psychology of running an SMB, showing the factors that influence owners’ purchasing decisions. Here are some key takeaways: Choose scalable solutions: Invest in technology that scales as you grow instead of upfront investments.Ease of use: Highlight your solutions as easy to learn and implement — even without any technical skills. TheU.S. Small Business Administration (SBA)offers theSBA Business Guideas a free resource on their website. This guide is designed to help you at every stage of your business journey. Learn fundamental topics like financing, legal requirements, and marketing. Here are some key takeaways: Step-by-step roadmap: Get guidance on everything from planning andstarting a businessto managing daily operations andgrowth strategies.Targeted support: Targeted programs for underserved groups, including women, veterans, and businesses inHUBZonesor rural areas. Service Corps of Retired Executives (SCORE)’sBusiness Mentorshipprogram provides free guidance to aspiring and existingentrepreneursthrough a nationwide network of volunteers and mentors. These experienced professionals offer advice and support across all stages of the business lifecycle, from initial planning to growth. Here are some key takeaways: Transparent mentorship: Be clear about your expectations and setbacks, as it helps your mentors offer accurate guidance and direction.Free templates: Quickly create abusiness plan, manage balance sheets, and conduct aStrengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, and Threats (SWOT)analysis.

inline_links.text: Small and Medium Business Trends Report

sections.heading: Free resources on marketing
sections.text: According to the latest State of Marketing Report,marketers use at least eight different tools and technologies in their business journey. Learning to market your products and the value they offer to your customers — all while being consistent across channels — is important. These free resources can get you there. Salesforce’sState of Marketing Reportis a survey of 5,000+ global marketers, who share insights aboutAI in marketing, personalization, andanalytics. It covers various concepts, making it a useful playbook for navigating the modern marketing landscape. Here are some key takeaways: Keep innovating: Learn and continuously innovate using AI to stand out in the market.Strong marketing ROI: Monitor sales pipeline and revenue to learn which campaigns are generatingconversionsandcustomer acquisition. Learn how to make moments magical with the web resourceHow Marketers Make Meaningful Moments Happenby Salesforce. This is a mix of demos,articles, webinars, podcasts, andcustomer storiesthat’ll guide you throughout your marketing journey. Bookmark this page for more learning. Here are some key takeaways: Foster loyalty: Build brand advocates and prioritizecustomer retentionby maintaining strong engagement with them.Personalize everything: Customers today expect highly-tailored and relevant experiences as it makes them feel heard and valued. Digital Marketing Roadmap Developmentis a Trailhead module that helps you assess your currentmarketing strategyand how to change it as you grow. Snack on this 25-minute course through topics like customer communication lifecycle and setting upspecific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound (SMART)metrics. Here are some key takeaways: Set key metrics: Whether your focus is onemail marketingorsocial media, identify thekey performance indicators (KPIs).Data quality matters: Your marketing performance is only as good as your data. Have the right and secure tools fordata management. Want to check out a podcast? Tune in toSalesforce Admins Podcastfor insights from industry leaders and experts. From tips on building your business to understanding which technologies can fuel your growth, this podcast features real-life stories and examples from Salesforce users and customers. Learn about problem-solving and delve into the “why” behind certain tools and processes. Here are some key takeaways: Evolve with the times: Learn about emerging trends in marketing, from AI to changing customer behaviors, to adapt and grow.Data-driven engagement Use data and technology to create personalized and meaningful customer engagement. AI for Marketersis a collection of free online courses and resources from Salesforce. This includes a range ofmarketing certificationsthat’ll equip you with the skills to thrive in this AI-driven world. You’ll also get acquainted withMarketing Cloudand how it’s useful for creatinglanding pagesand runningmultichannel campaigns. Here are some key takeaways: Email marketing practices: Understand how to set metrics likeopen ratesand engagement rates for your email marketing campaigns.Community learning: JoinMoment Marketer, a Trailblazer Community to interact with other marketers and stay up-to-date on marketing learnings from Salesforce. TheMarketing for Small Businessmodule on Trailhead is designed to help small business owners and marketers streamline their customer acquisition and engagement strategies. It focuses on using Salesforce’s specialized tools, such as Starter and Pro Suite, to automate repetitive marketing tasks, manage lead generation, and build personalized customer journeys without the need for a large marketing department. By the end of the module, users learn how to consolidate their marketing data to gain a “360-degree view” of their customers, allowing them to send the right message at the right time and ultimately scale their business more efficiently.

How Marketers Make Meaningful Moments Happen

sections.heading: Free resources on service
sections.text: At least63% of SMB leaders believe they meet customer expectations in comparison to large enterprises. Deliver consistent and prompt service to improve yourcustomer satisfaction rates. These free resources provide best practices and insights into keeping your customers happy with great service. Salesforce’sState of the AI Connected Customer Reportoffers advice from 16,000 consumers and business owners on howgenerative AI (Gen AI)is transforming customer experiences. This all-encompassing guide covers both perspectives, giving you deeper insights into both business and consumer behavior. Here are some key takeaways: Build trust: With an increase in AI usage, havingethical AIpractices in place will improve customers’ trust in your business. This increasesproduct adoption, too.Human involvement wins: Whether you’re using chatbots orautonomous AI agentswithAgentforce 360, human supervision is highly important. The Modern Customer Podcast, hosted by Forbes contributor and customer experience strategist Blake Morgan, spills the secrets of the world’s most customer-centric companies. Each weekly episode features experts and their advice on AI and leadership. Here are some key takeaways: Prioritize customer experiences: Advanced AI likemachine learning (ML)andnatural language processing (NLP)can help you understand customers better and automate mundane tasks.Customer-focused leadership: Effective customer experience starts from the top, requiring leaders to be deeply involved. Everything begins witheffective communication. And that’s what you’ll learn from the webinar,How to Use Slack and Salesforce to Win New Deals and Deliver Better Customer Service. Learn how to useSlackand Starter Suite together to improve your productivity and deliver better customer service. Here are some key takeaways: Communication consistency: Starter Suite is integrated with Slack and provides a unified view of your customers, letting your team communicate effectively about next steps in the sales process.Simplified operations: When Slack and Starter Suite work together, tasks likeproject managementand daily operations are more organized.

inline_links.text: 63% of SMB leaders believe they meet customer expectations in comparison to large enterprises
State of the AI Connected Customer Report
How to Use Slack and Salesforce to Win New Deals and Deliver Better Customer Service

sections.heading: Free resources on commerce
sections.text: When you’re not busy selling, you’re probably busy shopping. And that’s whyecommerceis having its moment. With these free resources, grow your commerce business and compete with big brands. Salesforce’sState of Commerce Reportoffers insights from 2,700 commerce leaders and 1.5 billion customers. Fromecommerce trendstodigital payments, customer needs are evolving. Learn all about these shifts and refine your strategies using this guide. Here are some key takeaways: Digital wallets are here: About62% of consumers prefer using digital wallets like Apple Pay, Amazon Pay, and Google Pay to buy online. Offer diverse payment options to make shopping more convenient.Social commerce roars: Consumers today shop from brands throughsocial media platforms. Engage them where they are. Start Selling Online with Storefronts in Starter, Pro, and Foundationsis a free webinar that covers various aspects of your ecommerce journey. From setting up youronline storefrontto runningecommerce merchandising, learn how Starter Suite andPro Suitecan help. Also, this webinar introduces you toSalesforce Foundations, a free CRM upgrade for existing Salesforce customers. This offers key features across Agentforce 360 and various Salesforce products likeSales CloudandService Cloud. Here are some key takeaways: It’s all about unified commerce: Bring your marketing, sales, and service together with built-in tools for a unified commerce strategy.Storefront builders make life easy: Even with minimal or no design experience, set up youronline storeusing ourstorefront builder. This comes integrated with Starter Suite. Sell Everywhere With Commerce Cloudis a comprehensive Trailhead module that covers all the basics of SalesforceCommerce Cloud. Get acquainted with its key features and plan yourunified commercestrategy and marketing efforts. You’ll need over three hours to complete this course. Here are some key takeaways: Unified customer experiences: Deliver consistent, personalized shopping experiences wherever your customers are engaging — on mobile, social media, in-store, or through chat.Simplify operations: Whether it’sinventory managementor running promotions, use a CRM that centralizes all your business and customer information. Cansocial commercegenerate sales? Absolutely. And that’s what you’ll learn inSocial Commerce Strategies. This Trailhead module teaches you aboutshoppable contentand building afeedback loopfor your social commerce strategy. You’ll need only 15 minutes to complete this course. Here are some key takeaways: Engaging shoppable content: Create content that resonates with your audience on social media and embed product links for shopping.Improve with feedback: Usesocial media monitoringto know how people are speaking about your business online. If you’ve been wondering how to prep for the holiday season, this quick guide will come in handy.Proven Tips to Make Every Season Your Peak Holiday Seasonis a free resource from Salesforce with top three tips to help you succeed during the holiday season. Learn how to deliver highly personalized shopping experiences using Agentforce 360, taking a cue fromSaks Global. Here are some key takeaways: Ecommerce site testing: Holiday site crashes cost sales – and your reputation. Test and optimize your ecommerce site for peak performance.Establish AI guardrails: This is the cornerstone ofethical and trusted operationsas it prevents unintended biases or harmful outcomes.

inline_links.text: 62% of consumers prefer using digital wallets like Apple Pay, Amazon Pay, and Google Pay to buy online
Start Selling Online with Storefronts in Starter, Pro, and Foundations
Proven Tips to Make Every Season Your Peak Holiday Season

sections.heading: Free resources on productivity
sections.text: Switching between tools and distractions are common culprits that impact productivity:SMB owners typically juggle four different tools, apps, or programs every workday. Get on track with these free resources. Salesforce’s free e-bookHow to Maximize Your CRM Productivityhelps you stay productive and scale fast with the right tools. It shares strategies to save time through automation, and boost employee efficiency. The guide also covers how a well-integrated CRM reduces costs and simplifies various business processes, from managingsales cyclestomarketing segmentation. And that’s whySalesforce CRM is increasing sales productivity by 30% and employee productivity by 31%. Here are some key takeaways: Enable self-service:Online portalswith answers to common concerns allow customers to troubleshoot issues, and manage their accounts independently.Train your team: Create custom learning paths or access free training on Trailhead to upskill your team. Personal Productivityis a Trailhead module on how to manage your time like a pro. Learn how to do your routine tasks in a new way and make your calendar work for you. Also, get tips on time blocking to work on high-priority tasks. You’ll need only 15 minutes to learn these skills. Here are some key takeaways: Do deep work: Turn off notifications, close down distractions, and give yourself the solid, unbroken time you need to‌ finish those big, important tasks.Set clear agendas: Set clear agendas for your meetings, and provide read-ahead documents to respect everyone’s time. Drive Productivity With Salesforce AIis a Trailhead module that covers everything from prompts to using out-of-the-box Salesforce AI products. Learn the basics ofprompt fundamentalsand how they can enhance operations across your business. Set up Agentforce 360 for your SMB and get all the required information onAgentforce Specialist Certification. Here are some key takeaways: Prompts simplify operations: Whether you’re usingAI prompts for salesor toimprove customer service, prompts guide AI to generate relevant and helpful responses.Save more time: Agentforce 360 helps you build AI agents to simplify operations by handling repetitive tasks and providing agents with the right information at the right time. How can AI impact sales and service across SMBs? InHow Small and Growing Businesses Can Use Generative AI to Drive Productivity, a Salesforce webinar, learn how AI enables personalization and efficiency across your business. This free resource also takes you through a quick demo. Here are some key takeaways: AI for lead management: Fromlead scoringtolead nurturing, AI helps you prioritize your most promising leads and recommends how to convert them fast.More alignment: A unified dashboard with AI insights creates more collaboration across your team.

inline_links.text: SMB owners typically juggle four different tools, apps, or programs every workday
How to Maximize Your CRM Productivity
Salesforce CRM is increasing sales productivity by 30% and employee productivity by 31%
How Small and Growing Businesses Can Use Generative AI to Drive Productivity

sections.heading: Free resources on AI
sections.text: A whopping87% of SMB leaders believe AI will equip them for the future. Keeping up with AI trends and learning is crucial for every SMB founder. The following resources will keep you updated with the latest and the best in AI. Grow Your Business With AI Agentsis a free e-book from Salesforce that highlights the key use cases of AI agents in an SMB. This covers the key components and types of AI agents that growing businesses often use. As most SMBs operate with lean resources, AI agents help them manage their growing business needs without increasing the headcount. Here are some key takeaways: Train your AI: Your AI agents are only as good as the data you train them with. So, keep training them on clean, updated data for data-driven decisions.Round-the-clock service: Unlike humans, AI agents don’t sleep and can deliver 24/7 service — no matter where, how, or when your customers contact you. Prefer to start with the fundamentals? InExplore Generative AI Tools, learn the basics oflarge language models (LLMs)andAI models. This Trailhead module covers AI capabilities and how it can understand ‌human conversational language. Today,AI chatbotsuse this as the foundation to deliver contextual and accurate customer service. Here are some key takeaways: Fine-tune your model: Based on your business needs and evolving goals, model fine-tuning enhances AI performance and accuracy.Fewer communication gaps: NLP helps computers understand and createhuman language, making communication between people and machines more natural and effective. With theAI Strategy Guide, learn how to prepare your business for and with AI, and the importance of having a strategic roadmap for it. This guide covers the necessity of setting AI guardrails and howGen AI guidelinescontribute to trustworthy AI development. From enabling your team ‌to having a solid checklist ready, these tips will get you started with ease. Here are some key takeaways: AI literacy: Continuous AI learning will help you adapt and stay relevant in our ever-changing world. Look for trusted resources likeSalesforce AI Researchto build knowledge.Foster trust: Building trust in AI is crucial as it improves adoption and enables safe, ethical integration of its benefits. How can SMBs effectively introduce AI into their operations? Find out in the webinarUnlocking Growth: AI and Tech Strategies for SMB Success in 2025. We cover how to use tech, particularly AI, as a catalyst in scaling your business. Also, understand the biggest challenges that SMBs are facing — along with their solutions. Here are some key takeaways: Increased optimism: SMB founders are feeling confident about their future, thanks to accessible technology and AI.Deep customer connections: SMBs feel they’re more connected with their customers, which gives them an advantage while delivering customized solutions. Voice AI agents are dramatically transforming customer experiences. InHow to Get Started With Voice AI, you’ll find out how. This comprehensive guide covers why automated agents aren’t enough for today’s consumers — and why they require voice AI agents to be on par with human service. The biggest benefit of voice AI agents is their ability to conductsentiment analysiswhile on call with their customers. They can customize their service based on the customer tone, and quickly escalate the calls to the appropriate department. Here are some key takeaways: Multilingual support: With voice AI agents, you can extend support in multiple languages, thus improving customer satisfaction.Better time management: Focus on high-priority and high-value tasks, since voice AI agents can seamlessly manage repetitive or low-value tasks. Everything you need to know about Salesforce Foundations is right insideGrow Your Business With Salesforce Foundations. This all-encompassing Trailhead module introduces you to the various features packed inside Foundations. This includes sales, marketing, service, and commerce. You’ll need about 14 hours to wrap up this course. Here are some key takeaways: Personalized marketing campaigns: Foundations gives a 360-degree view of your customers across various touch points, optimizing yourconversion rates.Data Cloud’s impact: Learn aboutData Cloudand how it works across the entire AI ecosystem at Salesforce. Thisfree e-bookis all about implementingAgentforce 360(Salesforce’s suite of autonomous AI agents) as a strategic move to scale without significantly increasing headcount. For SMBs, the transition from manual workflows to AI-driven automation doesn’t require a massive IT department. Salesforce outlines a clear five-step path to help smaller teams deploy autonomous agents that can plan, reason, and take action. TheSalesforce for Small Business YouTube channelserves as a dedicated resource hub for growing companies looking to simplify their operations. The channel features a mix of product tutorials, success stories, and expert tips focused on helping SMBs use marketing automation to find prospects, streamline quotes to win deals, and provide proactive customer service. A major highlight of the channel is its focus on Salesforce Explained, offering step-by-step setup guides that walk users through importing data, connecting email, and utilizing built-in AI tools.

inline_links.text: 87% of SMB leaders believe AI will equip them for the future
Grow Your Business With AI Agents
Unlocking Growth: AI and Tech Strategies for SMB Success in 2025
How to Get Started With Voice AI

We’re passionate about bringing you the latest trends and free tools designed to support your growth. Starter Suite is built for small and ambitious businesses like yours. Think of it as a launchpad, packed with friendly, built-in guidance and easy onboarding tutorials. Start your journey with theFreeorStarter Suitetoday. Looking for more customization? ExplorePro Suite. Already a Salesforce customer? ActivateFoundationsto try outAgentforce 360today. AI supported the writers and editors who created this article.

raw_html:
Key Takeaways

  • Learn over 30 free resources on sales, marketing, service, commerce, productivity, AI, and business strategy.
  • From reports on SMB tends to webinars on AI agents, these resources range from business strategy to technology innovations.
  • Free resources like this are helping businesses meet customer expectations 65% better than previous years.
This summary was created with AI and reviewed by an editor.

As a small and medium business (SMB) owner, you’re the CEO, social media manager, sales rep, and customer service representative all in one. If there’s one thing that can help you navigate these many roles, it’s knowledge. Always be learning, as the saying goes. 

Well, we’ve done the heavy lifting for you and compiled 30 free resources across all business teams — sales, marketing, service, and commerce — all with the added bonuses of productivity and artificial intelligence (AI). These valuable resources come from the experts, leaders, and innovators at Salesforce (along with some of our esteemed partners) built on trust, data, and all things AI. Let’s jump right in.

Free resources on sales and growth

Learning how to sell your products is the toughest and the most rewarding skill. These resources will equip you with information and tips on sales and business growth:

1. Saleforce’s Small and Medium Business Trends Report

Salesforce’s Small and Medium Business Trends Report is a go-to resource for global SMBs and startups for one reason: know what other founders are prioritizing. We spent over a year digging deep into this free report to help SMBs optimize their business for sustainable growth.

Get insights into AI tools for SMBs, top challenges, and tips to navigate them. And learn how AI agents can improve your business operations.

2. Trailhead: Sales for Small Business

Want to learn how to manage your sales pipeline or use automation in your sales journey? Sales for Small Business, a course on Trailhead, Salesforce’s free online learning platform, is your best bet. This short Trail requires only 30 minutes and introduces you to ‌customer relationship management (CRM) for small teams. 

Start with SMB Basics

Get the Free SMB Trends Report

How to Automate Your Small Business (AI Tips and Tools)

Win More Deals with Salesforce Starter Suite

Starter Suite vs. Pro Suite: CRM Comparison For SMBs

Here are some key takeaways:

  • Better lead management: A CRM for small business like Starter Suite helps you manage your leads, and focus on hot leads that will likely convert. 
  • Automation enhances productivity: Pro Suite can automate multiple tasks and boost your workflows and productivity.

3. State of Sales Report

Salesforce’s State of Sales Report draws insights from 5,500+ sales professionals, covering everything from AI adoption in sales to revenue-generating methods like upselling, and cross-selling. Learn about the top challenges that sales teams are facing, and how to address them. Here are some key takeaways:

  • Employee retention matters: Improve your team happiness and productivity through regular coaching and sales enablement on new tools, and AI. 
  • Good data matters: Sales teams rely on data for tasks like sales forecasting, setting targets, and quotas.

4. Trailhead: Salesforce for Small Business

Salesforce for Small Business is a Trailhead module on the benefits of a CRM for small and growing teams. In just 10 minutes, get an overview of SMBs’ impact on the economy and how Starter Suite equips them for the future. Here are some key takeaways:

  • CRM benefits: CRMs offer a 360-degree view of your sales and service operations, helping you make informed decisions.
  • Starter Suite’s scalability: Starter Suite grows as you scale, helping you seamlessly manage leads, contacts, and overall growth.

Trailhead: Salesforce for Small Business

Learn how your business can benefit from this out-of-the-box CRM.

Visit Trail
+200 points
Trail

Salesforce for Small Business

5. SMB Sales: A Complete Guide

Some say selling is an art, but we say it can be learned. The SMB Sales: A Complete Guide is a comprehensive resource on how to go after your prospects. It dives deep into the psychology of running an SMB, showing the factors that influence owners’ purchasing decisions. Here are some key takeaways:

  • Choose scalable solutions: Invest in technology that scales as you grow instead of upfront investments.
  • Ease of use: Highlight your solutions as easy to learn and implement — even without any technical skills.

6. Small Business Administration (SBA) Business Guide

The U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA) offers the SBA Business Guide as a free resource on their website. This guide is designed to help you at every stage of your business journey. Learn fundamental topics like financing, legal requirements, and marketing. Here are some key takeaways:

  • Step-by-step roadmap: Get guidance on everything from planning and starting a business to managing daily operations and growth strategies. 
  • Targeted support: Targeted programs for underserved groups, including women, veterans, and businesses in HUBZones or rural areas.

7. Service Corps of Retired Executives (SCORE) Business Mentorship

Service Corps of Retired Executives (SCORE)’s Business Mentorship program provides free guidance to aspiring and existing entrepreneurs through a nationwide network of volunteers and mentors. These experienced professionals offer advice and support across all stages of the business lifecycle, from initial planning to growth. Here are some key takeaways:

  • Transparent mentorship: Be clear about your expectations and setbacks, as it helps your mentors offer accurate guidance and direction.
  • Free templates: Quickly create a business plan, manage balance sheets, and conduct a Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, and Threats (SWOT) analysis. 

Boost sales with a CRM made for growing businesses

Try Starter Suite for free to close more deals and win more customers, today.

Try for free

Free resources on marketing

According to the latest State of Marketing Report, marketers use at least eight different tools and technologies in their business journey. Learning to market your products and the value they offer to your customers — all while being consistent across channels — is important. These free resources can get you there.

8. State of Marketing Report

Salesforce’s State of Marketing Report is a survey of 5,000+ global marketers, who share insights about AI in marketing, personalization, and analytics. It covers various concepts, making it a useful playbook for navigating the modern marketing landscape. Here are some key takeaways:

  • Keep innovating: Learn and continuously innovate using AI to stand out in the market.
  • Strong marketing ROI: Monitor sales pipeline and revenue to learn which campaigns are generating conversions and customer acquisition.

9. How Marketers Make Meaningful Moments Happen Guide

Learn how to make moments magical with the web resource How Marketers Make Meaningful Moments Happen by Salesforce. This is a mix of demos, articles, webinars, podcasts, and customer stories that’ll guide you throughout your marketing journey. Bookmark this page for more learning. Here are some key takeaways:

  • Foster loyalty: Build brand advocates and prioritize customer retention by maintaining strong engagement with them.
  • Personalize everything: Customers today expect highly-tailored and relevant experiences as it makes them feel heard and valued.

What can you do with a CRM built to reach more prospects?

Automate your marketing campaigns with a CRM built to grow. It all starts with Starter Suite.

Find out for free

10. Trailhead: Digital Marketing Roadmap Development

Digital Marketing Roadmap Development is a Trailhead module that helps you assess your current marketing strategy and how to change it as you grow. Snack on this 25-minute course through topics like customer communication lifecycle and setting up specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound (SMART) metrics. Here are some key takeaways:

  • Set key metrics: Whether your focus is on email marketing or social media, identify the key performance indicators (KPIs). 
  • Data quality matters: Your marketing performance is only as good as your data. Have the right and secure tools for data management. 

11. Salesforce Admins Podcast

Want to check out a podcast? Tune in to Salesforce Admins Podcast for insights from industry leaders and experts. From tips on building your business to understanding which technologies can fuel your growth, this podcast features real-life stories and examples from Salesforce users and customers. Learn about problem-solving and delve into the “why” behind certain tools and processes. Here are some key takeaways:

  • Evolve with the times: Learn about emerging trends in marketing, from AI to changing customer behaviors, to adapt and grow.
  • Data-driven engagement  Use data and technology to create personalized and meaningful customer engagement.

12. AI for Marketers Course

AI for Marketers is a collection of free online courses and resources from Salesforce. This includes a range of marketing certifications that’ll equip you with the skills to thrive in this AI-driven world. You’ll also get acquainted with Marketing Cloud and how it’s useful for creating landing pages and running multichannel campaigns. Here are some key takeaways:

  • Email marketing practices: Understand how to set metrics like open rates and engagement rates for your email marketing campaigns. 
  • Community learning: Join Moment Marketer, a Trailblazer Community to interact with other marketers and stay up-to-date on marketing learnings from Salesforce. 

Join millions in the growing Salesforce ecosystem

Trailblazers are free to reinvent themselves, free to dream bigger.

Be a Trailblazer

13. Trailhead: Marketing For Small Business

The Marketing for Small Business module on Trailhead is designed to help small business owners and marketers streamline their customer acquisition and engagement strategies. It focuses on using Salesforce’s specialized tools, such as Starter and Pro Suite, to automate repetitive marketing tasks, manage lead generation, and build personalized customer journeys without the need for a large marketing department.

By the end of the module, users learn how to consolidate their marketing data to gain a “360-degree view” of their customers, allowing them to send the right message at the right time and ultimately scale their business more efficiently.

Free resources on service
